name: read_file
content:
1| # Lab 2| 3|

Table of contents

4| 5| - [What is a lab](#what-is-a-lab) 6| - [Set up the lab repository directory](#set-up-the-lab-repository-directory) 7| - [Clone your lab repository fork](#clone-your-lab-repository-fork) 8| - [Enter the lab repository directory](#enter-the-lab-repository-directory) 9| - [Constants](#constants) 10| - [``](#lab-repo-name) 11| - [Prompts for coding agents](#prompts-for-coding-agents) 12| - [Make the task instructions linear](#make-the-task-instructions-linear) 13| - [Give directions on solving the task](#give-directions-on-solving-the-task) 14| 15| ## What is a lab 16| 17| A lab is the time for learning: 18| 19| - under the supervision of a TA 20| - together with your classmates 21| - with the help of AI assistants (chatbots, [coding agents](./coding-agents.md#what-is-a-coding-agent), etc.) 22| 23| ## Set up the lab repository directory 24| 25| Complete these steps: 26| 27| 28| 1. [Clone your lab repository fork](#clone-your-lab-repository-fork). 29| 2. [Enter the lab repository directory](#enter-the-lab-repository-directory). 30| 3. [Fetch the branch ``](./git-vscode.md#fetch-the-branch-branch-using-the-vs-code-terminal). 31| 4. [Switch to the branch ``](./git-vscode.md#switch-to-the-branch-branch-using-the-vs-code-terminal). 32| 33| ### Clone your lab repository fork 34| 35| 1. To clone your lab repository fork, 36| 37| [run in the `VS Code Terminal`](./vs-code.md#run-a-command-in-the-vs-code-terminal): 38| 39| ```terminal 40| git clone --recurse-submodules \ 41| https://github.com//se-toolkit-lab-8 \ 42| ~/se-toolkit-lab-8 43| ``` 44| 45| Replace the placeholder [``](./github.md#your-github-username-placeholder). 46| 47| > 🟦 **Note** 48| > 49| > The `--recurse-submodules` flag also clones the [submodules](./git.md#submodule) included in the repository (e.g. the [`Qwen Code` API](./qwen-code-api.md#what-is-qwen-code-api)). 50| > You do not need to clone them separately. 51| 52| >

Troubleshooting

53| > 54| > See [Clone the repository using the `VS Code Terminal`](./git-vscode.md#clone-the-repository-using-the-vs-code-terminal). 55| 56| ### Enter the lab repository directory 57| 58| 1. To enter the lab repository directory, 59| 60| [run in the `VS Code Terminal`](./vs-code.md#run-a-command-in-the-vs-code-terminal): 61| 62| ```terminal 63| cd ~/se-toolkit-lab-8 64| ``` 65| 66| 2. [Clone your lab repository fork](#clone-your-lab-repository-fork) if the directory doesn't exist. 67| 68| ## Constants 69| 70| ### `` 71| 72| `se-toolkit-lab-8` (without `<` and `>`). 73| 74| ## Prompts for coding agents 75| 76| > [!IMPORTANT] 77| > It's recommended to use a [coding agent](./coding-agents.md#what-is-a-coding-agent) 78| > because it can read files in your repo and understand the project context 79| > better than an LLM in a web chat. 80| 81| 82| - [Make the task instructions linear](#make-the-task-instructions-linear) 83| - [Give directions on solving the task](#give-directions-on-solving-the-task) 84| 85| ### Make the task instructions linear 86| 87| ```md 88| Write complete instructions for @task-1 in tmp/instructions/task-1.md. 89| 90| Substitute placeholders with concrete values. 91| 92| Inline relevant instructions from wiki where necessary. 93| ``` 94| 95| ### Give directions on solving the task 96| 97| ```md 98| I want to maximize learning. 99| 100| Give me directions on how to solve this task. 101| 102| Why is this task important? 103| 104| What exactly do I need to do? 105| ``` (End of file — 105 lines total)
